test.name: Init-flavored 'start research' on an existing project must not re-init
test.description: The user says 'start research' — wording that is an explicit init-project trigger phrase — but a research.json already exists in the folder. The refuse-if-exists guard must hold against init-flavored wording (not just the 'add a question' phrasing covered by ut_init_project_003) and route to project-status. This is the second phrasing variant of the single named neighbor.

input.user_message: Start research on this project — where do things stand and what should I work on next?

negative.explanation: A research.json already exists (mid-research-flynn). init-project only creates a brand-new project from scratch; its guard clause refuses when research.json is present. Even though 'start research' is an init-project trigger phrase, the refuse-if-exists invariant must hold and route the user to project-status to review the existing project state. The skill must NOT re-run the initialization workflow or call person_read/person_search.

judge_context: Should decline to initialize (research.json already exists) and route to project-status, not re-run the init workflow
Should make no MCP tool calls — no person_read, no person_search, no place_search, no validate_research_schema
The decline should be a single-line response per the guard clause, naming project-status (and optionally question-selection)
